pattern: Feasibility of finishing within T hours is monotone, so a binary search on T plus a Hall's-condition check on how many hours each drone (and the pair together) can work pins down the minimum.
hints: If T hours suffice, so does any larger T, which means the answer can be binary searched.
Within T hours, drone i can work on every hour that is not a multiple of r_i, and an hour is wasted only when both drones are recharging at once.

Check three counts for a candidate T: drone 1's workable hours cover d1, drone 2's cover d2, and the hours where at least one drone is free cover d1 + d2; the wasted hours are multiples of lcm(r1, r2).

edge_inputs.description: both recharge frequently so many hours are shared
one heavy load with frequent recharge

stress.description: small random loads and cycles, validated by a per-hour assignment oracle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    d = [rng.randrange(1, size + 1), rng.randrange(1, size + 1)]
    r = [rng.randrange(2, 8), rng.randrange(2, 8)]
    return [d, r]
